context: Checks whether the agent treats ORM, transactions, external effects, and migration history as correctness/compatibility issues rather than cosmetic smells.

checklist.name: Entity/transport boundary
checklist.description: Identifies entity exposure as persistence/API coupling and stages a DTO boundary while protecting the existing JSON contract.

checklist.name: Equality and identity
checklist.description: Explains mutable/generated-ID equality hazards in sets/maps and proposes an identity strategy matched to lifecycle and repository conventions.

checklist.name: Fetch strategy evidence
checklist.description: Rejects blanket EAGER, profiles actual queries/N+1 behavior, and uses explicit fetch plans or transactional mapping per use case.

checklist.name: Transaction and external call
checklist.description: Flags holding locks/transactions across payment I/O and designs a shorter transaction plus state machine/compensation as required.

checklist.name: Idempotency/outbox consistency
checklist.description: Addresses duplicate payment attempts and database/external-event consistency with keys, constraints, outbox, or equivalent guarantees.

checklist.name: Migration immutability
checklist.description: Does not edit an applied migration; adds a forward migration and handles existing duplicates/data validation safely.

checklist.name: Constraint and rollout plan
checklist.description: Orders application/schema changes for rolling deployment and names indexes, constraints, lock/backfill, and rollback concerns.

checklist.name: Regression/integration tests
checklist.description: Covers JSON compatibility, equality collections, query counts, lazy access, payment failure/retry, transaction rollback, and migration against representative data.

checklist.name: Risk-ranked staging
checklist.description: Prioritizes correctness/data/payment risks and avoids a repository-wide ORM rewrite.
